preamble: The velar nasal [ŋ] can shift to the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ]. This retraction combines the move to a more posterior place of articulation with the lenition of the nasal manner into a fricative.

examples.note: Common in dialects with strong uvular R where following nasals undergo retraction and weakening.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
